mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates robust household balance sheets and construction sector activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive return probability above 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns in greater than 50% of observations during periods where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth that supports corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth above 2.0% fails to produce a positive return frequency exceeding 55% over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation acceleration forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% MoM consistently coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces a negative return probability above 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside falling real personal income signals deteriorating consumer confidence and reduced capital expenditure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to generate negative returns in greater than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.
